official_description: Alar Pharmaceuticals is a new drug development company focusing on developing long-acting release drug products for central nervous system (CNS) disorders and chronic diseases. Aiming to submit the new drug application via the 505(b)(2) regulatory pathway and address the unmet medical needs. Long-acting injectables are under development of buprenorphine for opioid use disorder and animals’ chronic pain (osteoarthritis); ketamine for treatment-resistant depression (TRD) and fibromyalgia; apomorphine for Parkinson’s disease; a third-generation antipsychotic for schizophrenia, and other metabolic programs. Alar Pharmaceuticals has successfully completed a Phase 1 double-blind clinical trial of ALA-3000 in patients with TRD in the US (NCT06965569). This study was the first to demonstrate a long-acting ketamine formulation and characterized a sustained-release pharmacokinetic profile lasting for weeks following two subcutaneous administrations. The trial met its primary endpoint, showing a favorable safety and tolerability profile. Notably, the study reported no CNS adverse effects (AEs) typically associated with ketamine, such as sedation, dissociation, or psychosis-like symptoms. Additionally, no subjects discontinued or withdrew from the study due to AEs, and no clinically significant safety concerns were identified. There were no elevations in blood pressure, no signs of abuse potential, and no urinary-related AEs. Exploratory efficacy endpoints, including the MADRS, showed clinically meaningful improvements, further supporting the continued development of ALA-3000. ALA-3000 could potentially reduce the need for psychedelic-assisted psychotherapy, broadening accessibility to a wider patient population while reducing hospital burdens and improving patient compliance by eliminating mandatory prolonged post-dose monitoring. We believe this study marks a significant breakthrough in ketamine-based treatments for depression.

official_description: Amae Health is redefining outpatient care for people living with severe mental illness (SMI). We collaborate with leading health systems to deliver integrated, in-person specialty care designed specifically for individuals with conditions such as schizophrenia, schizoaffective disorder, bipolar disorder, treatment-resistant depression, and other complex psychiatric illnesses. At Amae Health, we believe progress in mental healthcare is not inevitable — it must be built intentionally through innovation, collaboration, and an unwavering commitment to reducing human suffering. We believe individuals living with serious mental illness deserve the same level of proactive, relationship-centered, measurement-driven care that exists for other chronic medical conditions. Our psychiatry-led clinics are intentionally designed to provide high-touch, multidisciplinary support tailored to the needs of this population. Teams include psychiatrists, therapists, primary care providers, dietitians, health coaches, peer mentors, community health workers, and care coordinators who work collaboratively to support both clinical outcomes and day-to-day functioning. In addition to evidence-based psychiatric treatment, members receive support navigating housing, employment, physical health, social connection, and life transitions often overlooked in traditional behavioral healthcare settings. Amae Health collaborates closely with major academic medical centers and health systems, including NewYork-Presbyterian, Cedars-Sinai, Mass General Brigham, CommonSpirit Health, and Novant Health, to expand access to specialized SMI care in local communities. Through these collaborations, we aim to reduce avoidable hospitalizations, improve continuity of care, and create more sustainable pathways to long-term recovery and stability. Technology and measurement-based care are foundational to our approach. We leverage clinical, behavioral, and outcomes data to better understand member progress and continuously improve care delivery. We believe technology, paired with compassionate clinical care, can become one of the most powerful tools for advancing mental health treatment and accelerating scientific discovery. In addition to delivering care, Amae Health is committed to advancing the future of SMI treatment through innovation, research partnerships, and workforce development. We are building a clinical environment where providers can practice deeply collaborative, mission-driven care while helping shape the future of psychiatry and behavioral health. Amae Health currently operates clinics across multiple U.S. markets and continues to expand access to comprehensive outpatient SMI services nationwide. We are actively seeking clinicians, healthcare operators, researchers, and partners who share our belief that mental healthcare can — and must — be done better.

official_description: Brain Health USA is a comprehensive mental health care provider located in Gardena, California, committed to serving individuals of all ages with a wide range of psychiatric and psychological services. With a professional team of over 200 board-certified psychiatrists and licensed mental health experts, the organization is known for its personalized, compassionate, and evidence-based approach to mental health care. The clinic offers a broad spectrum of services tailored to meet each patient's unique needs. These include detailed psychiatric evaluations, which help establish accurate diagnoses and inform individualized treatment strategies. Medication management is another core service, ensuring that patients receive the most effective pharmacological treatments with close monitoring. Brain Health USA also offers individual and group therapy, utilizing methods like Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) to treat conditions such as depression, anxiety, PTSD, and mood disorders. For children and adolescents, Brain Health USA provides specialized pediatric and adolescent psychiatric services, addressing developmental, behavioral, and emotional challenges. They also offer comprehensive ADHD testing and treatment plans, making them a go-to provider for families seeking support for attention-related disorders. In addition, the clinic provides substance use disorder treatments, including Suboxone therapy, to assist individuals on the path to recovery from opioid or other substance dependencies. Accessibility is a major focus for Brain Health USA. The clinic offers telehealth services, enabling virtual visits for patients who are unable to attend appointments in person, thereby increasing flexibility and maintaining continuity of care. Their extended office hours—open seven days a week from 7:00 AM to 9:00 PM—ensure that patients with various schedules can receive the help they need. To further support patients during times of acute distress, Brain Health USA provides 24-hour crisis line support, offering immediate assistance and guidance when it’s needed most. The clinic also accepts a wide variety of insurance plans, including Medi-Cal and Medicare, helping to remove financial barriers to quality mental health care. Beyond direct treatment, Brain Health USA is actively involved in community outreach and mental health education. They work to reduce stigma and promote awareness through workshops, support groups, and public engagement. This commitment to education and advocacy highlights their broader mission to create a more informed, empathetic society when it comes to mental health challenges. Patient feedback consistently reflects satisfaction with the level of care and professionalism at Brain Health USA. Testimonials often highlight the clinic’s ability to make patients feel heard, respected, and genuinely supported on their journey toward mental wellness. Their patient-centered model emphasizes trust, transparency, and long-term support.

official_description: Daybreak is a sleep health company transforming how obstructive sleep apnea (OSA) is identified and treated—especially for patients whose mental health, cognition, and quality of life are impacted by poor sleep. Sleep apnea is highly prevalent in psychiatric populations, yet remains significantly underdiagnosed and undertreated due to reliance on in-lab polysomnography, long wait times, and poor adherence to CPAP therapy. Daybreak was built to remove these barriers by delivering a modern, patient-centered pathway that is fully remote, evidence-based, and designed for real-world engagement. Daybreak offers an end-to-end, at-home solution for OSA, combining clinical screening, home sleep testing, sleep physician review, and treatment with custom oral appliance therapy for appropriate patients. Our model expands access to care while maintaining clinical rigor, enabling faster diagnosis and treatment without the friction of traditional sleep lab pathways. For psychiatrists and behavioral health providers, Daybreak serves as a trusted sleep partner—helping identify and treat a frequently overlooked contributor to mood disorders, anxiety, PTSD, cognitive impairment, and treatment-resistant depression. By addressing sleep apnea alongside psychiatric care, clinicians can support more comprehensive, whole-person outcomes. Daybreak’s platform is designed to integrate seamlessly into clinical workflows, offering clear referral pathways, transparent communication, and a high-touch patient experience. Our approach prioritizes patient adherence, long-term follow-up, and measurable outcomes—supporting both clinical confidence and patient satisfaction. With a national clinical network, scalable infrastructure, and a strong direct-to-consumer foundation, Daybreak is redefining sleep care beyond the sleep lab. We partner with providers, health systems, and government organizations to close care gaps and bring effective sleep apnea treatment to the patients who need it most. Better sleep isn’t optional—it’s foundational. Daybreak is proud to support mental health professionals in restoring sleep as a cornerstone of psychiatric care.

official_description: The Emotioncubes are a set of 12 solid-wood cubes that display information on typical aspects of human emotions. The information on each cube prompts clients to recognize their emotions, understand their meanings, and determine how they want to respond. At their simplest, Emotioncubes are a system for creating therapeutic conversations about emotions. The twelve Emotioncubes are: Angercube Disgustcube Envycube Fearcube Guiltcube Jealouscube Interestcube Lovecube Pridecube Sadcube Shamecube Discussioncube Every Emotioncube has six named and numbered faces. Eleven cubes have a specific order for their names and numbers (the Discussioncube is different) that help clients describe different aspects of their emotions and decide what to do next. The faces on these cubes are arranged as follows: 1. Words – to name what the emotion is. 2. Events – to identify what led to their emotion. 3. Thoughts – to recognize their thoughts related to the emotion. 4. Urges – to examine the urges they have in relation to the emotion. 5. Body Sensations – to feel where the emotion is in their body. 6. Choices – to decide how they want to respond to it. Emotioncubes are used to guide clients through conversations and help them identify, understand, and respond to their emotions. They are designed to be used flexibly, allowing therapists with different interests and skills to incorporate them into their preferred way of working.

official_description: Light Therapy Reinvented: Introducing Luminette® It is a pleasure to present a significant evolution in the field of chronobiology and clinical psychiatry: the Luminette®. As we deepen our understanding of the circadian rhythm’s role in mood disorders, the need for practical, evidence-based tools has never been more pressing.The Challenge of Traditional Light Therapy. For decades, light therapy has been a first-line treatment for Seasonal Affective Disorder (SAD) and a potent adjunct for non-seasonal depression, sleep-wake cycle disorders, and postpartum depression. However, traditional light boxes (10,000 lux) present a major clinical hurdle: compliance. Patients are often required to remain stationary for 30 to 60 minutes a day, a constraint that frequently leads to treatment discontinuation. The Luminette® Solution: Wearable Efficacy: Luminette® is an innovative pair of light therapy glasses designed to integrate seamlessly into a patient's morning routine. Developed through a collaboration between university researchers in sleep medicine and optical physics, this device bridges the gap between clinical efficacy and lifestyle compatibility. Key Technical Specifications: - Patented Optical System - Optimized Wavelength - Safety and Comfort Clinical Impact in Psychiatry: From a therapeutic standpoint, the Luminette® offers several advantages: - Enhanced Compliance: Patients can eat breakfast, brush their teeth, or work on a computer while receiving treatment, significantly increasing the likelihood of long-term adherence. - Precision Chronotherapy: The portability allows for precise "biological anchoring" in the early morning, which is crucial for phase-advancing patients with Delayed Sleep Phase Disorder or Bipolar II patients prone to seasonal dips. - Standardized Dosing: Three intensity settings allow clinicians to tailor the exposure time ($20$, $30$, or $60$ minutes) based on the patient’s sensitivity and the severity of symptoms. Conclusion: As we move toward more personalized and "ambulatory" psychiatric care, tools like the Luminette® empower patients to take an active, mobile role in their recovery. By aligning cutting-edge optical technology with the fundamental principles of circadian biology, we can offer a treatment that is as practical as it is profound.

official_description: Lumos Health Inc. is a Canadian health technology company developing wearable light therapy designed to better fit real life. Our first product, Lumos Glasses, delivers circadian-effective light in a glasses form factor, allowing users to receive light therapy while working, walking, cooking, or going about their day. For decades, light therapy has shown promise for supporting mood, sleep, and circadian health, but traditional light boxes can be inconvenient and difficult to integrate into everyday routines. Lumos was created to make light therapy more accessible, wearable, and practical for modern life. Our approach is grounded in circadian science. The human brain relies on specific wavelengths of light, especially in the blue-turquoise range, to help regulate sleep-wake timing, alertness, and mood. Yet modern indoor environments are often far dimmer and less biologically effective than natural daylight. Lumos Glasses were designed to help bridge that gap by bringing healthy daytime light into a familiar and usable format. In addition to daytime light delivery, Lumos also incorporates evening light management features intended to support healthier daily light patterns across the full circadian cycle. The system is paired with a companion app that allows users to control sessions, track usage, and better understand their light environment. Lumos was founded by Lucas Wen Tang, an engineer and inventor, and Dr. Jamie Zeitzer of Stanford Medicine, a leading researcher in sleep and circadian biology. The company’s work combines optical engineering, wearable hardware, app connectivity, and clinical research to rethink how light therapy can be delivered in daily life. Lumos is currently advancing clinical and commercial efforts aimed at improving how people support sleep, energy, and mental wellness through better light exposure. Our mission is to bring the benefits of circadian-effective light into a form that is elegant, usable, and aligned with the realities of everyday living.

official_description: Pause & Presence is a physician-led wellness company founded by Jessie Mahoney, MD, a pediatrician, mindfulness teacher, and certified physician coach. Her mission is to help healers heal themselves—and, in doing so, transform the culture of medicine. At a time when more than half of physicians report burnout, Pause & Presence offers an evidence-based and experiential approach to sustainable well-being. We create restorative spaces through retreats, workshops, and virtual coaching where physicians and healthcare teams can reconnect, realign, and rediscover meaning in their work and lives. Our programs are grounded in mindfulness, lifestyle medicine, and coaching science. Participants don’t just learn about well-being—they experience it. Designed by a physician, for physicians, every offering blends scientific rigor with reflection and practical tools to bring calm, clarity, and compassion to everyday practice. Whether retreats, group coaching programs for leaders or those pivoting, or institutional wellness workshops and programs—Pause and Presence experiences translate the science of burnout prevention into lived, embodied learning. All are accredited for CME credit through AKH, Inc. CME-accredited Women's Physician Wellness Retreats at Nicasio Creek Farm in West Marin integrate mindfulness, coaching, yoga, and culinary medicine in a natural setting. Leadership workshops and keynotes such as “Leading with Heart,” “The School of Unlearning,” and “Rewriting the Rules of Wellness and Influence in Medicine” help organizations build cultures of well-being and retention. Small group coaching programs such as “Deeper Connections,” “Mindful Love,” and “Transition Well” offer virtual circles for women physicians to apply mindfulness and mindset tools to real-life and medicine. Pause & Presence programs are based on research that shows professional coaching and mindfulness training can reduce emotional exhaustion and imposter syndrome, improve self-compassion and resilience, and increase satisfaction and engagement. Beyond the data, the impact is deeply personal—physicians rediscover presence, purpose, and peace. Jessie Mahoney, MD, practiced pediatrics and served as Chief of Physician Wellness at The Permanente Medical Group before founding Pause & Presence. A graduate of Dartmouth and UCSF, she now speaks nationally on healing the hidden curriculum of medicine, unlearning self-sacrifice, and creating careers. Her work has been featured by the American College of Cardiology, the American College of Lifestyle Medicine, and KevinMD. She also hosts the podcast "Healing Medicine: Mindfulness, Mindset & Physician Well-being." Visit Pause & Presence for a moment of calm amidst the conference bustle. Learn how mindfulness, coaching, and heart-centered leadership can help heal medicine from the inside out—one physician at a time.

official_description: Prime Medical Evaluators (PME) is a medical-legal services organization that specializes in delivering objective, evidence-based medical evaluations used in legal, insurance, and employment-related decisions. Operating primarily within the workers’ compensation and disability space, PME serves as a critical bridge between healthcare providers and stakeholders such as attorneys, insurance carriers, employers, and injured individuals. At its core, PME provides independent medical assessments designed to clarify complex medical issues in disputed or high-stakes cases. These include Qualified Medical Evaluations (QMEs), Independent Medical Examinations (IMEs), and Agreed Medical Evaluations (AMEs), all of which are essential in determining causation, impairment, disability status, and appropriate treatment. These evaluations play a central role in resolving disputes, guiding benefit determinations, and supporting fair legal outcomes. Beyond standard evaluations, PME offers a comprehensive suite of services that address a wide range of needs across the medical-legal spectrum. Functional Capacity Evaluations (FCEs) assess an individual’s physical abilities and limitations in relation to job requirements, while pre-employment and fitness-for-duty screenings help employers make informed hiring and return-to-work decisions. The organization also conducts peer reviews and medical record reviews to evaluate the quality, necessity, and appropriateness of prior medical care, often serving as expert support in litigation. A specialized area of focus for PME is Subsequent Injury Benefit Trust Fund (SIBTF) evaluations, which determine eligibility for additional benefits when a worker with a pre-existing condition sustains a new injury. These cases require a high level of medical and legal expertise, and PME has developed strong capabilities in delivering accurate and defensible assessments in this domain. PME operates through a large, multidisciplinary network of credentialed physicians and medical experts across various specialties. Each evaluator undergoes a rigorous vetting process and receives ongoing training to ensure consistency, compliance, and high-quality reporting. The organization emphasizes continuing education, particularly in medical-legal documentation and regulatory standards, to maintain the integrity and defensibility of its reports. In addition to clinical services, PME functions as a management and operational platform for physicians. It handles scheduling, case coordination, report processing, compliance, and administrative workflows, allowing doctors to focus on delivering high-quality evaluations. This infrastructure also enables efficient turnaround times and streamlined communication between all parties involved. A defining characteristic of PME is its commitment to ethical standards, impartiality, and professionalism. Every evaluation is conducted with the goal of producing clear, unbiased medical opinions that can withstand scrutiny in legal and regulatory settings. By combining clinical expertise with operational efficiency and legal awareness, PME delivers reliable, timely, and credible medical insights. Overall, Prime Medical Evaluators provides a comprehensive, end-to-end solution for medical-legal evaluation services, supporting informed decision-making and helping ensure fair and accurate outcomes across the healthcare and legal systems.

official_description: The Catatonia Foundation (TCF) is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to improving recognition, access to treatment, and outcomes for individuals with catatonia across the lifespan. Catatonia is a serious but treatable neuropsychiatric syndrome that occurs in mood disorders, psychotic disorders, autism and other neurodevelopmental disorders, medical conditions, and other settings. Despite well-established, first-line treatments — including benzodiazepines and electroconvulsive therapy (ECT) — catatonia remains widely under-recognized and inconsistently treated across psychiatric and medical environments. TCF works to close this gap through clinician education, research collaboration, patient education, patient advocacy education, and long-term systems change. Our mission is to work toward a future in which every person with catatonia receives timely recognition and appropriate first-line treatment. We develop accessible, clinically grounded educational resources for clinicians and families, providing practical guidance on recognition, diagnosis, and treatment considerations. We engage residency programs, professional organizations, and interdisciplinary healthcare teams to strengthen training and increase clinical confidence in identifying the full spectrum of presentations of catatonia across inpatient, outpatient, emergency, pediatric, geriatric, and general medical settings. In collaboration with psychiatrists, neurologists, emergency physicians, pediatric specialists, and researchers, TCF works to identify persistent knowledge gaps and structural barriers to care, and to translate those findings into targeted educational initiatives, clinician resources, and collaborative dialogue. We are working to improve clarity and consistency in terminology, encourage stronger integration of catatonia education into mainstream psychiatric training, and promote improved data collection to better understand prevalence, presentation, and outcomes. Catatonia occurs across diagnostic and medical contexts — yet expertise remains limited. There are very few clinicians nationally with meaningful experience in catatonia, and access to those clinicians is limited. This reflects a broader systemic gap between the need for informed, timely care and the availability of experienced providers. Patients frequently encounter delayed recognition, fragmented treatment pathways, and avoidable complications — even though appropriate intervention often produces rapid and significant improvement. TCF addresses these challenges by strengthening clinical education, promoting collaboration across disciplines, and equipping patients and families with reliable information. We provide structured educational tools to help patients understand catatonia and navigate healthcare systems effectively. We also provide advocacy education that helps families communicate clearly with providers and advocate for timely evaluation and appropriate treatment. We believe meaningful progress requires sustained collaboration — across disciplines, institutions, and lived experience — to improve recognition, strengthen systems of care, and advance more timely and effective treatment wherever patients present.

official_description: The Menninger Clinic is a non-profit nationally ranked leader in psychiatric care affiliated with Baylor College of Medicine, providing a full continuum of mental health services for adolescents and adults. For more than a century, Menninger has been a trusted resource for individuals and families seeking expert care when mental health concerns feel complex, persistent, or difficult to resolve. We specialize in serving individuals with complex mental health needs, including mood and anxiety disorders, trauma-related conditions, personality disorders, substance use disorders, co-occurring disorders, and treatment-resistant illnesses. Menninger is often a meaningful next step for individuals who have not experienced lasting improvement with prior treatment, those seeking deeper clarity around their diagnosis, or individuals navigating a psychiatric crisis for the first time. Care at Menninger is grounded in thoughtful psychiatric assessment and individualized treatment planning. Our programs are designed to meet individuals where they are in their recovery journey, offering varying levels of structure and support based on clinical need. Treatment options include inpatient and residential care, as well as step-down and transitional levels of care that help individuals gradually build stability, skills, and independence. Our integrated continuum allows individuals to transition thoughtfully between levels of care while maintaining clinical continuity and therapeutic momentum. Onsite specialized services, including brain stimulation therapies and sleep medicine, further enhance our ability to individualize treatment and address co-occurring clinical needs that may impact recovery. Our expert interdisciplinary clinical teams include psychiatrists, psychologists, social workers, therapists, nurses, and other mental health professionals who work collaboratively to deliver coordinated, evidence-based treatment. In addition, we work in collaboration with patients, families, and referring clinicians to ensure shared understanding, continuity of care, and alignment around immediate goals and long-term recovery. At Menninger, treatment extends beyond symptom management. We focus on helping individuals gain insight into their mental health, strengthen coping strategies, improve daily functioning, and develop the skills necessary for sustained recovery beyond treatment. Through compassionate support and clinical expertise, we help individuals move forward with greater clarity, confidence, and hope, supporting meaningful progress and improved quality of life.

official_description: Zenara Health builds clinician-first infrastructure that makes comprehensive assessment and measurement-based care practical in real psychiatric workflows. Founded by Ravi Hariprasad, MD— a practicing psychiatrist—Zenara Assist works as a clinical sidecar alongside your existing EHR (no rip-and-replace) to help practices get to clearer diagnosis and better follow-through with less administrative burden. Zenara Assist includes three modules: Assess: a structured, patient-guided intake that produces a clinician-ready portrait before the first visit—so the first encounter can focus on formulation and treatment planning rather than fact-finding. Assess generates structured outputs that support CPT-aligned documentation for common behavioral health workflows, helping practices standardize intake and reduce repeated data-gathering across visits. Monitor: makes measurement-based care routine by supporting validated symptom tracking (e.g., PHQ‑9/GAD‑7) and presenting outcomes longitudinally as “mental vitals.” Measurement-based care is increasingly supported in psychiatry as a practical way to guide treatment decisions and improve care quality when implemented consistently Care: supports between-visit orchestration—tracking tasks, outreach, and team coordination so essential follow-through work is visible, organized, and auditable. This aligns with evidence-based care models such as collaborative care, where structured monitoring and coordinated workflows are core components of successful implementation. Zenara serves psychiatrists in solo and group practice, behavioral health teams, and primary care clinics integrating behavioral health.
